name: Evanston
vibe: Northwestern University's home, with a dense, walkable downtown and lakefront parks.
description: Evanston is the North Shore's most urban suburb, combining Northwestern's campus, a Metra and CTA-connected downtown, and its own lakefront beaches, making it a common landing spot for people who want suburban schools without giving up walkability.
commute: CTA Purple Line + Metra Union Pacific North, roughly 25-35 minutes to the Loop
schools: Evanston Township High School (District 202); highly rated District 65 elementary schools
photoCredit: Downtown Evanston skyline, photo by Nutmegger, via Wikimedia Commons
funFacts: Home to Northwestern University's main campus.
Evanston was the birthplace of the Woman's Christian Temperance Union in 1874 and remained a dry town — no alcohol sales — until 1972.
distanceFromLoop: About 12 miles north of the Loop — roughly 25-30 minutes by car, 30-35 minutes via the Purple Line or Metra

name: Wilmette
vibe: Classic North Shore suburb with the Baha'i Temple, top-rated schools, and lakefront beaches.
description: Wilmette is a quintessential North Shore family suburb, known for New Trier High School's reputation, tree-lined streets, and Gillson Park's beach and harbor.
commute: Metra Union Pacific North, roughly 30-40 minutes to the Loop
schools: New Trier High School; District 39 elementary

name: Winnetka / Kenilworth
vibe: Among the wealthiest suburbs in the country, with large lots and elite public schools.
description: Winnetka and neighboring Kenilworth represent the top of the North Shore market, with large historic homes, extensive lakefront parks, and New Trier High School consistently ranked among the top public schools nationally.
commute: Metra Union Pacific North, roughly 35-45 minutes to the Loop
famousResidents: Ann-Margret — actress, New Trier High School alumna
Ivan Albright — painter and printmaker, New Trier High School alumnus
funFacts: The house used as the McCallister family home in Home Alone (1990) is a real private residence on Lincoln Avenue.
Winnetka's median household income exceeded $250,000 in 2023.
rankings: 2nd-richest place in Illinois — Bloomberg's Richest Places Annual Index, 2019
distanceFromLoop: About 16 miles north of the Loop — roughly 30-35 minutes by car, 35-45 minutes via Metra Union Pacific North

name: Highland Park
vibe: Ravine-cut streets, Ravinia Festival's outdoor concerts, and a walkable downtown.
description: Highland Park is known for the Ravinia Festival (the Chicago Symphony Orchestra's summer home), a mix of architecture from Prairie-style to contemporary, and a downtown with its own Metra stop.
commute: Metra Union Pacific North, roughly 45-55 minutes to the Loop
schools: Highland Park High School; District 112 elementary

name: Oak Park
vibe: The largest concentration of Frank Lloyd Wright buildings anywhere, and the closest walkable suburb to the city.
description: Oak Park borders the city directly and offers CTA train service into the Loop, making it feel like an extension of the city while adding larger lots, its own school district, and the country's densest collection of Frank Lloyd Wright and Prairie School architecture.
commute: CTA Green Line + Metra Union Pacific West, roughly 20-30 minutes to the Loop
schools: Oak Park and River Forest High School; District 97 elementary
photoCredit: Frank Lloyd Wright Home and Studio, via Wikimedia Commons
famousResidents: Ernest Hemingway — born and raised in Oak Park
Frank Lloyd Wright — lived and worked in Oak Park for 20 years, designing many of his early Prairie School buildings there
funFacts: Home to the largest concentration of Frank Lloyd Wright-designed buildings anywhere in the world.
distanceFromLoop: About 9 miles west of the Loop — roughly 20-25 minutes by car, 25-30 minutes via the Green Line

name: River Forest
vibe: Oak Park's quieter, more residential neighbor along the Des Plaines River.
description: River Forest shares Oak Park's architectural pedigree and high school district but trades density for larger, quieter residential lots along the river.
commute: Metra Union Pacific West, roughly 25-30 minutes to the Loop
schools: Oak Park and River Forest High School

name: La Grange
vibe: A compact, walkable downtown with two Metra lines and a strong small-town feel.
description: La Grange punches above its size with a genuinely walkable downtown of shops and restaurants, served by two separate Metra lines, making it popular with commuting families who still want a main-street feel.
commute: Metra BNSF or Metra Heritage Corridor, roughly 25-30 minutes to the Loop
schools: Lyons Township High School; District 102 / 105 elementary

name: Hinsdale
vibe: One of the western suburbs' most affluent communities, known for large lots and new construction teardowns.
description: Hinsdale combines a historic downtown with a significant amount of new-construction luxury homes built on teardown lots, and is consistently ranked among the top school districts in Illinois.
commute: Metra BNSF, roughly 30-35 minutes to the Loop
photoCredit: Downtown Hinsdale Historic District, photo by Teemu08, CC BY-SA 3.0, via Wikimedia Commons
funFacts: Downtown Hinsdale is a National Register Historic District, largely unchanged despite the intense teardown-and-rebuild activity on surrounding residential blocks.
distanceFromLoop: About 15 miles west of the Loop — roughly 25-30 minutes by car, 35-40 minutes via Metra BNSF

name: Naperville
vibe: One of the largest and most amenity-rich suburbs in the country, with a Riverwalk downtown.
description: Naperville is consistently ranked among the best places to raise a family nationally, with a park district-run Riverwalk, two top-rated high school districts, and enough scale to have its own restaurant and retail ecosystem separate from the city.
commute: Metra BNSF, roughly 40-50 minutes to the Loop
schools: Naperville Central / Naperville North High Schools
famousResidents: Evan Lysacek — Olympic gold medalist figure skater
Candace Parker — WNBA champion and Olympic gold medalist
funFacts: Naperville's Riverwalk, built by volunteers in 1981 for the city's 150th anniversary, is widely credited as the turnaround point for its downtown.
rankings: #1 Best City to Live in America — Niche.com, 2025 and 2026
Best Public Schools in America — Niche.com
No. 16, Best Places to Live — Money Magazine, 2022
distanceFromLoop: About 28 miles west of the Loop — roughly 40-50 minutes by car, 60-70 minutes via Metra BNSF

name: Downers Grove
vibe: A well-regarded, slightly more affordable alternative to Hinsdale and Naperville.
description: Downers Grove offers strong schools and a walkable downtown at a lower price point than neighboring Hinsdale, drawing families who want the same commuter rail access without the premium.
commute: Metra BNSF, roughly 35-40 minutes to the Loop
schools: Downers Grove North / South High Schools

name: Arlington Heights
vibe: A walkable downtown built around its Metra station, plus the site of the former Arlington Park racetrack now slated for major redevelopment.
description: Arlington Heights has one of the northwest suburbs' most successful transit-oriented downtowns, and is at the center of a major long-term redevelopment plan for the former Arlington International Racecourse site.
commute: Metra Union Pacific Northwest, roughly 40-50 minutes to the Loop
photoCredit: Arlington Heights Historic District, via Wikimedia Commons
funFacts: Was known as 'Dunton,' after original settler William Dunton, until 1874.
Formerly home to The Cellar (1964-1970), a teen rock club that hosted acts including The Who, The Byrds, and Cream.
distanceFromLoop: About 25 miles northwest of the Loop — roughly 40-45 minutes by car, 50-60 minutes via Metra Union Pacific Northwest

name: Schaumburg
vibe: A major retail and corporate hub anchored by Woodfield Mall.
description: Schaumburg is one of the region's largest job and retail centers outside the city, home to Woodfield Mall and a large concentration of corporate offices, making it popular for buyers who work locally rather than commuting downtown.
commute: Metra Milwaukee District West (nearby Schaumburg/Roselle stops), roughly 50-60 minutes to the Loop

name: Park Ridge
vibe: One of the closest suburbs to the city and O'Hare, with a classic Midwestern downtown.
description: Park Ridge borders the city and sits minutes from O'Hare, giving it an unusually short commute for a suburb along with a walkable downtown and strong schools.
commute: Metra Union Pacific Northwest, roughly 30 minutes to the Loop

name: Elmhurst
vibe: A leafy, close-in suburb with a compact downtown and its own art museum.
description: Elmhurst sits close to O'Hare and the city, with a walkable downtown, the Elmhurst Art Museum, and consistently strong schools, at a lower price point than Hinsdale.
commute: Metra Union Pacific West, roughly 30 minutes to the Loop

name: Orland Park
vibe: The southwest suburbs' commercial and residential hub, with newer housing stock than most Chicago suburbs.
description: Orland Park offers relatively new housing stock, a large retail base, and a well-regarded park district, making it one of the most popular destinations for families relocating to the southwest suburbs.
commute: Metra SouthWest Service, roughly 50-60 minutes to the Loop
schools: Carl Sandburg / Victor J. Andrew High Schools

name: Tinley Park / Frankfort
vibe: Two of the fastest-growing southwest suburbs, with a steady supply of newer subdivisions.
description: Tinley Park and Frankfort have seen significant new-construction growth over the past two decades, offering larger homes on larger lots than closer-in suburbs, at some of the best price-per-square-foot in the metro.
commute: Metra Rock Island / SouthWest Service, roughly 55-65 minutes to the Loop

name: Oak Lawn
vibe: One of the closest southwest suburbs to the city, with an established, affordable housing stock.
description: Oak Lawn offers one of the shorter commutes among southwest suburbs along with a large stock of well-established ranch and split-level homes at accessible price points.
commute: Metra SouthWest Service, roughly 35-40 minutes to the Loop

name: Homer Glen / Lockport
vibe: Larger lots and a more rural feel at the outer edge of the southwest suburbs, along the historic I&M Canal.
description: Homer Glen and Lockport sit further from the city but offer noticeably larger lots and lower price points, with Lockport's historic downtown along the I&M Canal adding some small-town character.
commute: Metra Heritage Corridor (limited schedule) or car commute, roughly 60+ minutes to the Loop
